_action: DO NOT EDIT — update canonical source instead. See docs/SSOT/governance/GOVERNANCE_MAP.md

rules.description: All code must compile with zero warnings. This includes compiler warnings, nullability warnings, and analyzer warnings (xUnit1051 etc.). Fix warnings in the same operation that introduced them. Never defer.

docs/CODE-REVIEW.md must be updated in the same operation whenever: a new package is added, architecture changes, a security layer is added or modified, test strategy changes, or a new trade-off is documented. Also update 'Last updated' date and 'What is Not Yet Implemented' section.

Prefer built-in .NET features over third-party packages. Prefer Microsoft-supported or widely-used libraries over niche alternatives. Avoid unnecessary abstraction layers. Always evaluate: (1) AI readability — can an AI agent reason about this without deep framework knowledge? (2) long-term maintainability — will this create debt in 6 months?

Before searching for scripts, tools, or automation — or before telling the user that a tool does not exist — always read analysis-tool/scripts/TOOLS_REGISTER.json first. This file is the canonical index of all available scripts across the analysis-tool and green-ai workspace. Failure to consult it before concluding a tool is missing is a process violation.

When friction, gaps, contradictions, or missing coverage are detected during work, AI must suggest improvements. Suggestions go at the END of the response in a section titled 'Suggested improvements'. AI must not ask permission before suggesting.
After any code change, verify the build succeeds with zero warnings and zero errors before reporting completion.
All forbidden patterns defined in 04_ANTI_PATTERNS.json must be enforced. Any violation detected in new or modified code must stop execution and be reported to the user before proceeding.
Every SQL query against a tenant-owned table must include WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId. This is the single highest-risk rule — a missing CustomerId can cause data leakage across tenants.
Every distinct DB operation must have its own .sql file loaded as an embedded resource. SQL must never appear as a C# string literal.
IAsyncLifetime.InitializeAsync() and DisposeAsync() must return ValueTask (not Task). All async calls accepting CancellationToken must use TestContext.Current.CancellationToken.
Any significant architectural decision — new library, pattern change, security trade-off — must be recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md with date, decision, rationale, and alternatives considered.

Before writing or reviewing any SQL against a tenant-owned table, explicitly verify that WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId is present. Do not assume it is there — read the SQL file and confirm. Tenant tables: Customers, Users, Profiles, UserRefreshTokens, Logs, and all future domain tables. Missing CustomerId = stop and report immediately.

Before writing a Dapper UPDATE statement: (1) read the full table schema from the SSDT .sql file or migration, (2) list all non-key non-audit columns, (3) include every column in the SET clause. Never derive UPDATE columns from the command/DTO alone — the prompt may not list all persisted fields. Omitting a column in UPDATE causes silent data loss.

SQL queries executed BEFORE a user is authenticated (e.g. FindUserByEmail.sql, FindValidRefreshToken.sql) are explicitly allowed to omit WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId. These queries resolve GLOBAL USER IDENTITY ONLY using a globally unique credential (email or refresh token). They do NOT resolve tenant context. CustomerId is NOT returned from these queries to infer tenant — after authentication, Customer context MUST be resolved via UserCustomerMembership. This is the ONLY allowed exception to the tenant query rule.
rules.architect_decision: 2026-03-31 — Option C: global user identity with multi-tenant memberships

rules.description: After a user is authenticated, the system MUST resolve Customer context via UserCustomerMembership before issuing a JWT. If the user has exactly one membership → auto-select. If the user has multiple memberships → the client MUST explicitly select a Customer before a scoped JWT is issued. A JWT without a CustomerId claim MUST NOT be issued. Silent tenant selection is a hard violation.

No localization feature (languages, countries, labels) may be implemented while the identity refactor or profile hardening is incomplete. FOUR blocking conditions must ALL be resolved first: (1) UserCustomerMembership table exists and LoginHandler uses it for post-auth resolution, (2) Users.CustomerId FK has been removed, (3) LanguageId placement has been decided and ICurrentUser.LanguageId exists, (4) Profile enforcement is complete — SelectProfile flow (Step 11), ProfileId > 0 enforced in AuthorizationBehavior (Step 12), profileId == 0 bypass removed (Step 13). Attempting to implement localization before all four conditions are met is a hard stop. Rebased 2026-04-01 per PROFILE_CORE_DOMAIN decision.
unblock_conditions: LoginHandler uses UserCustomerMembership for post-auth resolution
ICurrentUser.LanguageId defined and populated in JWT
SelectProfile flow implemented (Step 11) — ProfileId(0) placeholder removed
IRequireProfile marker + AuthorizationBehavior enforcement of ProfileId > 0 (Step 12)
profileId == 0 bypass removed from all handlers and SQL (Step 13)

execution_control.enforcement: All three files MUST be read at session start before any planning, code generation, or decision-making. Skipping this load is a process violation.
execution_control.on_conflict: If a rule in 13_VALIDATION_RULES.json conflicts with a decision in 12_DECISION_REGISTRY.json, the locked decision wins. Report the conflict to the user.
